name: Flipkart-Style Product Import Pipeline — JPA Persistence
scenario: Upgrade the in-memory product catalog from Topic 13.2 to use real database persistence with Spring Data JPA. Import a batch of products, store them in H2, and serve them through the existing REST API. Add derived queries for searching and filtering.
description: Full stack: @Entity Product → ProductRepository (with derived queries) → @Transactional ProductService → @RestController ProductController. H2 datasource configured. Batch import via CommandLineRunner. @DataJpaTest for repository layer.

core.description: `Product` @Entity: id (UUID auto-generated), name (not null, max 200), price (not null), category (not null), stockQty (not null), createdAt (updatable=false). Protected no-arg constructor. Full-arg constructor.

`ProductRepository extends JpaRepository<Product, String>` with 4 derived queries: findByCategory, findByPriceLessThanEqual, findByNameContainingIgnoreCase, existsByName. Plus one @Query: count distinct categories.
`ProductService` (@Service @Transactional): importProducts(List<ProductRequest>), findAll(), findById(id), search(query), findByCategory(cat), getStats() → count, avgPrice, categories.
`ProductController` (@RestController @RequestMapping('/api/products')): GET all, GET/{id} (200/404), GET/search?q=..., GET/category/{cat}, GET/stats.
`ProductImportRunner` (@Component implements CommandLineRunner): imports 5 products at startup using ProductService. Prints count and total inventory value after import.
`application.properties`: H2 in-memory, create-drop, show-sql=true, h2-console enabled, port=8090.
@DataJpaTest tests: save+findById, findByCategory, findByPriceLessThanEqual, existsByName, and the custom @Query count.

bonus.description: Add `@Modifying @Transactional @Query` to bulk-update prices by category: `updatePricesByCategory(double factor, String category)`. Test with @DataJpaTest.
Add a second @Entity `Category` with @OneToMany to Product. ProductRepository: findByCategoryEntity. Migrate the import runner to use Category entities.
Add pagination: `ProductController.getAll(@RequestParam int page, @RequestParam int size)` using `PageRequest.of(page, size)` and `ProductRepository.findAll(Pageable)`. Return `Page<Product>`.

data_to_use:
Import 5 products at startup:
P001: Wireless Mouse, ₹799, Electronics, qty=50
P002: Mechanical Keyboard, ₹1999, Electronics, qty=12
P003: USB-C Hub, ₹499, Accessories, qty=60
P004: Monitor 27", ₹18999, Electronics, qty=5
P005: Ergonomic Chair, ₹8999, Furniture, qty=8

Derived queries to test:
findByCategory('Electronics') → 3 products
findByPriceLessThanEqual(1000) → 2 products (Mouse + Hub)
findByNameContainingIgnoreCase('key') → 1 product
countDistinctCategories → 3
example_output:
.   ____          _            __ _ _
...
Hibernate: create table products (...)
[ProductImportRunner] Importing 5 products...
Hibernate: insert into products ...
[ProductImportRunner] Imported 5 products
[ProductImportRunner] Total inventory value: ₹2,041,755
Started Application in 2.8 seconds

# REST endpoints:
GET  /api/products          → [{5 products}]
GET  /api/products/P001     → {Wireless Mouse}
GET  /api/products/search?q=key → [{Keyboard}]
GET  /api/products/category/Electronics → [{3 products}]
GET  /api/products/stats    → {count:5, avgPrice:6259, categories:3}
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key patterns: @Entity requires protected no-arg constructor. JpaRepository<Product, String> gives free CRUD. @DataJpaTest rolls back each test — use @BeforeEach to seed. JPQL uses field names (not column names). @Query for countDistinctCategories: 'SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) FROM Product p'.
submission_instructions: Push to GitHub. `mvn spring-boot:run` and verify all endpoints with curl or Postman. Screenshot the H2 console showing the products table.
tips_for_success: @Column(updatable=false) on createdAt: set in constructor, Hibernate won't update it
@DataJpaTest: each test method gets a rollback — seed in @BeforeEach
